title: NOC 44101 – Home Support Workers, Caregivers, and Related Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Home Support Workers, Caregivers, and Related Occupations under NOC 44101 provide essential personal care and assistance to seniors, persons with disabilities, and individuals requiring support at home. Classified under TEER 4, this occupation is one of the most in-demand caregiving roles in Canada. These professionals typically work in:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER4 – Occupations usually require a secondary school diploma or several weeks of on-the-job trainingMajor Group44 – Care providers and public protection support occupationsSub-Major Group441 – Home care provider occupationsMinor Group4410 – Home care provider occupationsNOC Code44101

common_job_titles: Attendant for persons with disabilities – home care
duties: Provide care and companionship during illness or incapacity
Assist with bathing, dressing, grooming, and mobility
Plan and prepare meals and special diets
Feed or assist in feeding clients
Perform light housekeeping duties such as:LaundryDishwashingBed-making
Care is typically delivered in a private home setting.
employment_requirements: Completion of caregiver or personal support worker training may be required
First aid and CPR certification may be required
Some provinces may require additional training or registration.
salary_prospects: $35,000 – $46,000 per year,with higher wages available in northern territories.
benefits: In addition to wages, benefits may include:
Employer contributions to CPP/QPP and Employment Insurance
pathways: Home Support Workers may qualify under caregiver immigration programs, depending on eligibility criteria such as:
Caregiver programs are subject to policy updates and intake caps.
Clear pathway to permanent residence (subject to eligibility)
If you are planning to work in Canada as a Home Support Worker or Caregiver, contact GTR Immigration today to explore your pathway to Canada PR under NOC 44101.
additional_information: Elderly individualsPersons with disabilitiesIndividuals recovering from illness or surgeryFamilies experiencing temporary disruption
This occupation plays a major role in Canada’s caregiver immigration programs, including:
Home Child Care Provider Pilot (HCCP)Home Support Worker Pilot (HSWP)
